title: Charred Crunchy Ukrainian Barbeque Sauce Braised
description: A sick-day Ukrainian dish featuring charred, crunchy textures and a sweet, tangy barbeque sauce. This egg-free-friendly dish is perfect for a rainy day.
instructions: Season the pork belly with salt, black pepper, paprika, and crushed red pepper.

In a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot, heat the infuse-oil over medium-high heat. Sear the pork belly until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side. Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook the sliced onion until caramelized, stirring occasionally, about 10-12 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, and barbeque sauce to the pot. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Return the pork belly to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
While the pork is braising, prepare the crunchy cornflake crumbs by pulsing them in a food processor until coarsely ground.
Remove the pot from the oven and carefully remove the pork from the pot, letting it cool slightly.
Shred the pork into bite-sized pieces and return it to the pot. Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and chopped fresh dill.
Transfer the pork to a serving dish and top with the crunchy cornflake crumbs.
Drizzle with additional barbeque sauce, if desired.
